addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

3151	void ZopfliVerifyLenDist(const unsigned char *data, size_t datasize, size_t pos,
3152	                         unsigned short dist, unsigned short length)
3153	/* The whole input buffer of `datasize` bytes must be a valid, readable object. */
3154	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(data, datasize))
3155	/* The forward window [pos, pos+length) must lie within the buffer.  Phrased as
3156	   `pos <= datasize` and `length <= datasize - pos` so the bound is checked
3157	   without the overflow that `pos + length <= datasize` would risk when `pos`
3158	   is close to SIZE_MAX.  This is the condition the function itself asserts. */
3159	__CPROVER_requires(pos <= datasize)
3160	__CPROVER_requires((size_t)length <= datasize - pos)
3161	/* The back-reference must not point before the start of the buffer, otherwise
3162	   the index `pos - dist + i` would underflow (size_t wraparound). */
3163	__CPROVER_requires((size_t)dist <= pos)
3164	/* A back-reference always carries a DEFLATE match length, which never exceeds
3165	   ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH.  This lets the constant-bounded quantifier below cover every
3166	   iteration the loop can run. */
3167	__CPROVER_requires((size_t)length <= (size_t)ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH)
3168	/* The back-reference window must actually match the forward window, otherwise
3169	   the inner `assert(data[pos - dist + i] == data[pos + i])` would fail.  The
3170	   SAT backend only supports quantifiers with *constant* bounds, so the range is
3171	   the constant [0, ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH] with the symbolic `k < length` moved inside
3172	   as a guard (per the CBMC quantifier documentation).  The indexing matches the
3173	   loop body so CBMC instantiates this against the loop's accesses. */
3174	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
3175	    size_t k; (0 <= k && k < ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH) ==>
3176	        (k < length ==> data[pos - dist + k] == data[pos + k]) })
3177	/* The function only reads from `data`; it has no observable side effects. */
3178	__CPROVER_assigns()
3179	{
3180	
3181	    /* TODO(lode): make this only run in a debug compile, it's for assert only. */
3182	    size_t i;
3183	
3184	    assert(pos + length <= datasize);
3185	    for (i = 0; i < length; i++)
3186	    {
3187	        if (data[pos - dist + i] != data[pos + i])
3188	        {
3189	            assert(data[pos - dist + i] == data[pos + i]);
3190	            break;
3191	        }
3192	    }
3193	}

3197	#define HASH_SHIFT 5

3199	/*
3200	Update the sliding hash value with the given byte. All calls to this function
3201	must be made on consecutive input characters. Since the hash value exists out
3202	of multiple input bytes, a few warmups with this function are needed initially.
3203	*/
3204	static void UpdateHashValue(ZopfliHash *h, unsigned char c)
3205	/* The hash object itself must be a valid, writable object. */
3206	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
3207	/* The running hash value is always the result of a previous masking with
3208	   HASH_MASK (or zero-initialized), hence in [0, HASH_MASK]. This invariant
3209	   guarantees that `h->val << HASH_SHIFT` (at most HASH_MASK << 5 = 1048544)
3210	   does not overflow the signed `int`. */
3211	__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3212	/* Only the current hash value is updated. */
3213	__CPROVER_assigns(h->val)
3214	/* The masking re-establishes the [0, HASH_MASK] invariant for the next call. */
3215	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3216	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == ((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ (c)) & HASH_MASK))
3217	{
3218	    h->val = (((h->val) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ (c)) & HASH_MASK;
3219	}

3221	/*
3222	Prepopulates hash:
3223	Fills in the initial values in the hash, before ZopfliUpdateHash can be used
3224	correctly.
3225	*/
3226	void ZopfliWarmupHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
3227	                      ZopfliHash *h)
3228	/* The hash object itself is a valid, writable object. */
3229	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
3230	/* UpdateHashValue's precondition: the running hash value is the result of a
3231	   previous HASH_MASK masking (or zero-initialized), hence in [0, HASH_MASK]. */
3232	__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3233	/* The input data is a readable buffer of `end` bytes. The unconditional read at
3234	   array[pos] is kept in bounds by pos < end; the array[pos + 1] read is guarded
3235	   by pos + 1 < end. */
3236	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, end))
3237	/* The warmup position lies within the data, as at every call site. */
3238	__CPROVER_requires(pos < end)
3239	/* Only the running hash value is updated (via UpdateHashValue). */
3240	__CPROVER_assigns(h->val)
3241	/* The running hash value stays masked into [0, HASH_MASK], preserving the
3242	   invariant the next call (and UpdateHashValue) relies on. */
3243	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3244	{
3245	    UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 0]);
3246	    if (pos + 1 < end)
3247	        UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 1]);
3248	}

3370	                      ZopfliHash *h)
3371	/* The hash object itself is a valid, writable object. */
3372	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
3373	/* UpdateHashValue's precondition: the running hash value is always the result of
3374	   a previous HASH_MASK masking (or zero-initialized), hence in [0, HASH_MASK]. */

3377	   values, which are masked into [0, HASH_MASK], so HASH_MASK + 1 entries suffice. */

3379	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, (size_t)(HASH_MASK + 1) * sizeof(*h->head2)))

3381	   either the empty marker -1 or a valid window slot in [0, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE),
3382	   which keeps the chained `hashval[head[val]]` / `hashval2[head2[val2]]` reads
3383	   (guarded by `!= -1`) in bounds. That invariant can only be written as a
3384	   `__CPROVER_forall` over each 32768-entry table; under the SAT backend such a
3385	   constant-bound quantifier is expanded into one conjunct per index, forcing the
3386	   whole table to be concretized. With both tables that makes verification time
3387	   out, and it gates no extra checked property here: the head index feeding each
3388	   chained read is the running hash *after* UpdateHashValue, an arbitrary value in
3389	   [0, HASH_MASK] that no precondition over the prior state can pin down. The
3390	   memory-layout (`is_fresh`) and functional invariants below are what this
3391	   contract verifies. */

3394	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->hashval)))

3396	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->prev)))

3409	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev),
3410	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2),
3411	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->same),
3412	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head),
3413	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2))

3421	
3422	    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH <= end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);

3424	    if (h->head[h->val] != -1 && h->hashval[h->head[h->val]] == h->val)
3425	    {
3426	        h->prev[hpos] = h->head[h->val];
3427	    }
3428	    else
3429	        h->prev[hpos] = hpos;

3432	    /* Update "same". */

3455	    }
3456	    else
3457	        h->prev2[hpos] = hpos;
3458	    h->head2[h->val2] = hpos;
3459	}

3461	void ZopfliResetHash(size_t window_size, ZopfliHash *h)
3462	/* window_size is bounded by the actual window used by all callers; the upper
3463	   bound also keeps the index i within unsigned short range, so prev[i] == i. */

3467	   window_size entries, matching ZopfliAllocHash. */
3468	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, (size_t)65536 * sizeof(*h->head)))
3469	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, (size_t)65536 * sizeof(*h->head2)))
3470	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, window_size * sizeof(*h->prev)))
3471	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, window_size * sizeof(*h->hashval)))
3472	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, window_size * sizeof(*h->same)))
3473	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, window_size * sizeof(*h->prev2)))
3474	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, window_size * sizeof(*h->hashval2)))
3475	__CPROVER_assigns(h->val, h->val2,
3476	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head),
3477	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2),
3478	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev),
3479	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval),
3480	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->same),
3481	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2),
3482	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval2))

3484	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
3485	    size_t ka; (ka < 65536) ==> (h->head[ka] == -1 && h->head2[ka] == -1) })
3486	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
3487	    size_t kb; (kb < window_size) ==>
3488	        (h->prev[kb] == kb && h->prev2[kb] == kb &&
3489	         h->hashval[kb] == -1 && h->hashval2[kb] == -1 && h->same[kb] == 0) })

3491	    size_t i;
3492	
3493	    h->val = 0;
3494	    for (i = 0; i < 65536; i++)
3495	    __CPROVER_assigns(i, __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head))
3496	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= 65536)
3497	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(__CPROVER_forall {
3498	        size_t k; (k < i) ==> (h->head[k] == -1) })
3499	    {
3500	        h->head[i] = -1; /* -1 indicates no head so far. */
3501	    }
3502	    for (i = 0; i < window_size; i++)
3503	    __CPROVER_assigns(i, __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev), __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval))
3504	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= window_size)
3505	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(__CPROVER_forall {
3506	        size_t k; (k < i) ==> (h->prev[k] == k && h->hashval[k] == -1) })

3510	    }
3511	
3512	    for (i = 0; i < window_size; i++)
3513	    __CPROVER_assigns(i, __CPROVER_object_whole(h->same))
3514	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= window_size)
3515	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(__CPROVER_forall {
3516	        size_t k; (k < i) ==> (h->same[k] == 0) })
3517	    {
3518	        h->same[i] = 0;
3519	    }
3520	
3521	    h->val2 = 0;
3522	    for (i = 0; i < 65536; i++)
3523	    __CPROVER_assigns(i, __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2))
3524	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= 65536)
3525	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(__CPROVER_forall {
3526	        size_t k; (k < i) ==> (h->head2[k] == -1) })
3527	    {
3528	        h->head2[i] = -1;
3529	    }
3530	    for (i = 0; i < window_size; i++)
3531	    __CPROVER_assigns(i, __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2), __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval2))
3532	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= window_size)
3533	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(__CPROVER_forall {
3534	        size_t k; (k < i) ==> (h->prev2[k] == k && h->hashval2[k] == -1) })
3535	    {
3536	        h->prev2[i] = i;
3537	        h->hashval2[i] = -1;
3538	    }

3541	static void FollowPath(ZopfliBlockState *s,
3542	                       const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
3543	                       unsigned short *path, size_t pathsize,
3544	                       ZopfliLZ77Store *store, ZopfliHash *h)
3545	{
3546	    size_t i, j, pos = 0;
3547	    size_t windowstart = instart > ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE
3548	                             ? instart - ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE
3549	                             : 0;
3550	
3551	    size_t total_length_test = 0;
3552	
3553	    if (instart == inend)
3554	        return;
3555	
3556	    ZopfliResetHash(ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE, h);
3557	    ZopfliWarmupHash(in, windowstart, inend, h);
3558	    for (i = windowstart; i < instart; i++)
3559	    {
3560	        ZopfliUpdateHash(in, i, inend, h);
3561	    }
3562	
3563	    pos = instart;
3564	    for (i = 0; i < pathsize; i++)
3565	    {
3566	        unsigned short length = path[i];
3567	        unsigned short dummy_length;
3568	        unsigned short dist;
3569	        assert(pos < inend);
3570	
3571	        ZopfliUpdateHash(in, pos, inend, h);
3572	
3573	        /* Add to output. */
3574	        if (length >= ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH)
3575	        {
3576	            /* Get the distance by recalculating longest match. The found length
3577	            should match the length from the path. */
3578	            ZopfliFindLongestMatch(s, h, in, pos, inend, length, 0,
3579	                                   &dist, &dummy_length);
3580	            assert(!(dummy_length != length && length > 2 && dummy_length > 2));
3581	            ZopfliVerifyLenDist(in, inend, pos, dist, length);
3582	            ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(length, dist, pos, store);
3583	            total_length_test += length;
3584	        }
3585	        else
3586	        {
3587	            length = 1;
3588	            ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(in[pos], 0, pos, store);
3589	            total_length_test++;
3590	        }
3591	
3592	        assert(pos + length <= inend);
3593	        for (j = 1; j < length; j++)
3594	        {
3595	            ZopfliUpdateHash(in, pos + j, inend, h);
3596	        }
3597	
3598	        pos += length;
3599	    }
3600	}

3602	/*
3603	Calculates the optimal path of lz77 lengths to use, from the calculated
3604	length_array. The length_array must contain the optimal length to reach that
3605	byte. The path will be filled with the lengths to use, so its data size will be
3606	the amount of lz77 symbols.
3607	*/
3608	static void TraceBackwards(size_t size, const unsigned short *length_array,
3609	                           unsigned short **path, size_t *pathsize)
3610	{
3611	    size_t index = size;
3612	    if (size == 0)
3613	        return;
3614	    for (;;)
3615	    {
3616	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(length_array[index], path, pathsize);
3617	        assert(length_array[index] <= index);
3618	        assert(length_array[index] <= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH);
3619	        assert(length_array[index] != 0);
3620	        index -= length_array[index];
3621	        if (index == 0)
3622	            break;
3623	    }
3624	
3625	    /* Mirror result. */
3626	    for (index = 0; index < *pathsize / 2; index++)
3627	    {
3628	        unsigned short temp = (*path)[index];
3629	        (*path)[index] = (*path)[*pathsize - index - 1];
3630	        (*path)[*pathsize - index - 1] = temp;
3631	    }
3632	}

3635	{
3636	    return a < b ? a : b;

31	typedef struct ZopfliLZ77Store
32	{
33	    unsigned short *litlens; /* Lit or len. */
34	    unsigned short *dists;   /* If 0: indicates literal in corresponding litlens,
35	         if > 0: length in corresponding litlens, this is the distance. */
36	    size_t size;
37	
38	    const unsigned char *data; /* original data */
39	    size_t *pos;               /* position in data where this LZ77 command begins */
40	
41	    unsigned short *ll_symbol;
42	    unsigned short *d_symbol;
43	
44	    /* Cumulative histograms wrapping around per chunk. Each chunk has the amount
45	    of distinct symbols as length, so using 1 value per LZ77 symbol, we have a
46	    precise histogram at every N symbols, and the rest can be calculated by
47	    looping through the actual symbols of this chunk. */
48	    size_t *ll_counts;
49	    size_t *d_counts;
50	} ZopfliLZ77Store;
51	
52	typedef struct SplitCostContext
53	{
54	    const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77;
55	    size_t start;
56	    size_t end;
57	} SplitCostContext;
58	
59	/* Number of distinct literal/length and distance symbols in DEFLATE */
60	#define ZOPFLI_NUM_LL 288
61	
62	#define ZOPFLI_NUM_D 32
63	
64	typedef struct SymbolStats
65	{
66	    /* The literal and length symbols. */
67	    size_t litlens[ZOPFLI_NUM_LL];
68	    /* The 32 unique dist symbols, not the 32768 possible dists. */
69	    size_t dists[ZOPFLI_NUM_D];
70	
71	    /* Length of each lit/len symbol in bits. */
72	    double ll_symbols[ZOPFLI_NUM_LL];
73	    /* Length of each dist symbol in bits. */
74	    double d_symbols[ZOPFLI_NUM_D];
75	} SymbolStats;
76	
77	typedef struct RanState
78	{
79	    unsigned int m_w, m_z;
80	} RanState;
81	
82	/*
83	Function that calculates a cost based on a model for the given LZ77 symbol.
84	litlen: means literal symbol if dist is 0, length otherwise.
85	
86	Instance: `GetCostFixed` using NULL as context
87	Instance: `GetCostStat` using `SymbolStats` as context
88	*/
89	typedef double CostModelFun(unsigned litlen, unsigned dist, void *context);
90	
91	typedef struct ZopfliHash
92	{
93	    int *head;            /* Hash value to index of its most recent occurrence. */
94	    unsigned short *prev; /* Index to index of prev. occurrence of same hash. */
95	    int *hashval;         /* Index to hash value at this index. */
96	    int val;              /* Current hash value. */
97	
98	    /* Fields with similar purpose as the above hash, but for the second hash with
99	    a value that is calculated differently.  */
100	    int *head2;            /* Hash value to index of its most recent occurrence. */
101	    unsigned short *prev2; /* Index to index of prev. occurrence of same hash. */
102	    int *hashval2;         /* Index to hash value at this index. */
103	    int val2;              /* Current hash value. */
104	
105	    unsigned short *same; /* Amount of repetitions of same byte after this .*/
106	} ZopfliHash;
107	
108	typedef struct Node Node;

This is a deeply complex function. Before writing, let me record my feasibility analysis: `FollowPath` calls `ZopfliFindLongestMatch` and `ZopfliStoreLitLenDist` inside a loop, and those callees' contracts impose **position-specific / size-parity preconditions that cannot hold across loop iterations** (e.g. `limit <= 6` vs. `length` up to 258, `sublen` must be a fresh 8-element array but `FollowPath` passes `NULL`, `store->size` must be non-power-of-two yet it increments every iteration, and the in-body `assert(dummy_length == length ...)` needs the real match semantics the contract abstracts away).

Let me write the strongest sound memory-safety contract I can and run CBMC once to capture the concrete diagnostic.

static void FollowPath(ZopfliBlockState *s,
                       const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
                       unsigned short *path, size_t pathsize,
                       ZopfliLZ77Store *store, ZopfliHash *h)
/* ---- input window ----
   `in` is a readable buffer of `inend` bytes.  Every read (the warmup/update
   walk over [windowstart, instart), the literal read in[pos], and the
   back-reference verification) is at an index strictly below `inend`. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(in, inend))
/* The start of the region to encode is at or before the end, matching the
   caller (instart..inend is the byte range of this run). */
__CPROVER_requires(instart <= inend)
/* ---- path ----
   `path` holds `pathsize` lengths; path[i] is read for i in [0, pathsize). */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(path, pathsize * sizeof(*path)))
/* ---- hash object ----
   The hash and all of its sub-arrays must be valid, writable objects.  head and
   head2 are the full 65536-entry hash-to-index tables (ZopfliAllocHash); the
   per-window-slot arrays each hold ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE entries.  These sizes are
   the union of what ZopfliResetHash, ZopfliWarmupHash, ZopfliUpdateHash and
   ZopfliFindLongestMatch require of the hash. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, (size_t)65536 * sizeof(*h->head)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, (size_t)65536 * sizeof(*h->head2)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->prev)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->prev2)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->hashval)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->hashval2)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->same)))
/* ---- block state and longest-match cache ----
   `s` and its cache must be valid objects for ZopfliFindLongestMatch. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc, sizeof(*s->lmc)))
/* ---- output store ----
   The store and all of its parallel arrays must be valid objects for
   ZopfliStoreLitLenDist. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
